detailed_description: This artifact is a screenshot of an Ignition web status page for a local gateway, specifically the Systems > Overview / performance view. The page shows dashboard-style health and performance widgets including CPU, memory, threads, CPU trend, memory trend, and system response. A green banner near the top states that the license is incomplete and includes a reset-trial control. The visible metrics appear relatively stable at the moment captured, with low CPU usage, a memory value around 2,561 MB, current response time of 11 ms, and no recent slow response events displayed. The screenshot appears to be diagnostic evidence because it documents the runtime state of the Ignition gateway rather than an action being taken.

short_description: Windows Event Viewer screenshot showing a Service Control Manager error for the OptiSweep service terminating unexpectedly and an automatic restart action.
detailed_description: This artifact is a screenshot of Windows Event Viewer with the System log selected. An Event Properties dialog is open for Event 7031 from Service Control Manager. The visible message states that the OptiSweep service terminated unexpectedly, had done so 1 time, and that corrective action would be taken in 60000 milliseconds by restarting the service. The event details pane shows Level as Error and includes a computer name visible as wcsupsfwtxignition. The surrounding event list shows multiple Service Control Manager entries, including the highlighted error at 2/20/2026 6:36:02 AM and nearby informational entries.
visual_observations: The screenshot shows Windows Event Viewer with Windows Logs > System selected.
An Event Properties window is open for 'Event 7031, Service Control Manager'.
The General tab is selected in the Event Properties window.
The message text visible in the dialog says the OptiSweep service terminated unexpectedly.
The message also states that corrective action will be taken in 60000 milliseconds to restart the service.
The event details visible in the dialog include Log Name: System, Source: Service Control Manager, Event ID: 7031, and Level: Error.
A computer name is visible in the event details as wcsupsfwtxignition.
The event list behind the dialog contains many Service Control Manager entries, with one error row highlighted around 2/20/2026 6:36:02 AM.
The right-side actions pane includes entries such as Event Properties, Attach Task To This Event, Copy, Save Selected Events, Refresh, and Help.
contextual_interpretation: This screenshot appears to document a service crash event rather than a successful steady-state condition.
The visible text indicates Windows was configured to attempt an automatic restart of the OptiSweep service after the unexpected termination.
Because the screenshot shows the error event and planned corrective action, it is useful as diagnostic evidence of the failure condition and partial recovery handling.
The image does not confirm from this screenshot alone whether the restart ultimately succeeded.
